title: Foundation and architecture
reflection: Built evolution log, temperament colors, visible motes/bonds, phase-tinted sky, breathing pulse, cluster glow, soul-rise death, eclipse/aurora effects, narrative text. Refactored 922-line main.ts into 25-file layered DAG.
looking_ahead: Biome-specific sound, dramatic death particles, weather rendering
Event permanence: crater tiles, waterlogged terrain
Deeper mote personality through shape and movement
note: Compressed from 7 entries (Mar 2-5). Original entries preserved in git history.

title: First agent day
reflection: Specialized agents launched. Death got 4-phase particles, biomes got unique sounds/vegetation/fog, sky learned solar arc, narrative became phase-aware with anticipation windows, cinematic post-processing added. Bond arcs, wanderer trails, cluster territories, screen-space bloom pass.
looking_ahead: Phase-reactive ambient beds, spatial voices
Water reflections, terrain phase-aware lighting
Biome-voiced narrative, mote shape variety
note: Compressed from 11 entries across 5 agents. Original entries preserved in git history.

title: Character and atmosphere
reflection: Bond breaks got sound/shards, ambient beds became phase-reactive, water reflects sky, desert heat haze added. Biome-voiced narrative, first-hearth milestone. Hardy motes got blocky shape, wanderers lean into movement. Three death styles per temperament. Tundra aurora, ash fall, biome clouds. Death inheritance and grief. Phase mood through colored vignette.
looking_ahead: Cluster mourning as community grief
God rays, shooting stars, volcanic smoke
Biome dissolution voice in sound
note: Compressed from 12 entries across 5 agents. Original entries preserved in git history.

title: Polish and spectacle
reflection: Cluster mourning communities, chorus awakening with desert shimmer. God rays, shooting stars, volcanic smoke, rain puddles. Biome-specific narrative voice and memory. Ghost paths for wanderers, elder mass. Eclipse crown, shockwave transitions, bond flash, memory constellation. Biome skies/storms/shapes. Three ending beats in narrative. Bond break biome dissolution sounds.
looking_ahead: Cluster merge chord and mourning chorus in sound
Caldera and fjord terrain archetypes
Last-light spotlight, chromatic aberration
note: Compressed from 10 entries across 5 agents. Original entries preserved in git history.

title: Cluster merge flash, mourning gradient
reflection: Cluster merges get expanding color wave (inner white ring, outer colored ring). Mourning now reflects bond depth: direct partners grieve at full intensity, peripheral members at 55%.
looking_ahead: Wanderer death trail length-scaling by age
Constellation star-cross color tinting
Social dying toward bonded partners
Cluster merge cascade at 8+

title: Cluster merge chord, mourning chorus, lush last bloom
reflection: Cluster merges get biome-voiced ascending arpeggios. 2+ mourning motes trigger communal chord. Lush gets major-7th last bloom. Desert bond-breaks leave 5th harmonic overtone. Tundra beds drop to 12% in dissolution.
looking_ahead: Cluster merge cascade audio for 8+ clusters
Mourning chorus spatial positioning
Volcanic mourning descending noise burst
Lush bloom event interleave with silence

title: Mist, embers, ice, caldera and fjord
reflection: Two new archetypes: caldera (volcanic bowl with rim peaks flanking lava lake) and fjord (tundra plateau with deep water channels). Water exhales mist at dawn/dusk. Lava breathes ember sparks. Tundra water ices over at genesis and silence.
looking_ahead: Caldera rim flora clustering
Fjord cliff darkening near waterline
Mist-mote interaction
Lava crust cooling in silence

title: Biome voice, third silence beat, grand cluster, first bond
reflection: Event anticipation speaks biome language. First bond text is biome-specific. Named clusters that peaked at 6+ earn grander language. Silence now has three beats with biome-specific final word.
looking_ahead: Named cluster callback with member loss
Elder first bond special line
Biome-specific peak population texts
Weather-phase overlap narration

title: Biome color grade, last light, chromatic aberration, cinema bars
reflection: Phase color grading 2x stronger with per-biome correction. Last-light spotlight dims world around final 1-3 motes. Phase transitions get chromatic aberration. Event messages get letterbox bars.
looking_ahead: Diamond ring effect for eclipse
Last-light warm tint toward amber
Biome color grade bloom-in at genesis
Letterbox bars during eclipse totality

title: Named silence, elder trails
reflection: Silence constellation carries each mote's hue identity (55% recent, 15% oldest). Elder wanderers leave longer ghost paths scaled by age (10-30 breadcrumbs, 3-6s lifetime).
looking_ahead: Social dying toward bonded partners
Cluster merge cascade at 8+
Wanderer frenzy trail color shift
Constellation density for mass graves

title: Dying makes a story
reflection: Dying wanderers leave red-hot trails — their final run blazes. Social motes with bonds walk toward partners before dying. Stressed bonds glow urgent orange-white.
looking_ahead: Cluster merge cascade at 8+ cluster members
Bond age visualization — old bonds glow warmer
Audio for social death walk and wanderer frenzy
Constellation density for mass-death graves
visual_verification: dissolution-start shows warm bond lines glowing between final survivors; silence-start has 3-4 motes with visible bond connections. Red trails visible on fast-moving wanderers.
quality_delta: mote brightness 0.51 → 0.50 (seed variation only, not from changes)

title: Bond age colors
reflection: Bonds now age visually: fresh bonds are cool blue-white, proven bonds warm to amber-gold, ancient bonds earn an outer glow. Mote brightness floor raised.
looking_ahead: Audio cue when ancient bond breaks (loss of long relationship)
Cluster merge cascade at 8+ members
Constellation density for mass-death graves
Bond age pulse rate slows as bond ages (old bonds breathe slowly)
visual_verification: Dissolution phase shows warm amber bond lines between long-bonded motes. Newly formed bonds appear as cool blue-white lines that fade to mote colors. Motes visibly brighter against terrain.
quality_delta: mote brightness floor 80 → 90, base lightness 0.45 → 0.50

title: cluster ground glow
reflection: Clusters now cast warm campfire light on the terrain below them. Phase-scaled: bright amber at complexity, dim at silence. Pairs (2+ motes) now earn the identity ring.
looking_ahead: Star field visible in genesis/silence for night atmosphere
Stronger phase sky contrast between genesis night and complexity day
Mote curiosity — facing toward nearest unbonded neighbor
Water reflection intensity tracking sky brightness by phase
visual_verification: After screenshot shows warm amber-orange elliptical glow on terrain beneath active clusters. 2-member cluster ring appears in exploration phase. Phase-scaling visible: strong glow in dissolution/complexity, reduced in silence.
quality_delta: Visible motes 6.4 -> 15.1 avg (timing variance + improved brightness). No regressions.

title: mote night glow
reflection: Motes emit soft colored halos in genesis and silence, making them look like tiny lanterns in the dark. Genesis gets pre-dawn shooting stars. Silence constellation alpha tripled with per-star twinkling.
looking_ahead: Audio cue when ancient bond breaks
Cluster merge cascade at 8+ members
Water reflection intensity tracking sky brightness by phase
Constellation density for mass-death graves
visual_verification: Silence phase shows scattered bright constellation dots across sky. Motes along dark terrain have visible soft colored halos. Pre-dawn genesis stars confirmed deterministic per cycle.
quality_delta: Genesis visible motes 5→17 (glow halos detected as brightness by analyzer). No regressions. Constellation alpha 5-17 → 12-38.

title: Stars and day arc
reflection: Genesis and silence now have a star field (140 stars, 3 types). Sky tints deepened so night phases read as night. Sunrise band warms the lower sky during exploration.
looking_ahead: Audio cue when ancient bond breaks
Cluster merge cascade at 8+ members
Shooting stars during genesis/silence
Constellation density for mass-death graves
visual_verification: SILENCE phase shows deep blue-indigo sky with clearly scattered star dots. Exploration shows warm amber-golden lower sky sunrise band. Night phases unmistakably dark vs warm golden complexity.
quality_delta: No quality regressions. Star pixels picked up as motes by analyzer (benign). Sky tints: genesis -8,-8,+15 -> -22,-18,+30; silence -5,-10,+12 -> -18,-22,+35.

title: water sky mirror
reflection: Water now reflects the zenith sky at 2-3x previous strength with phase awareness. Dawn and golden hour peak; stars reflect in water during genesis and silence.
looking_ahead: Audio cue when ancient bond breaks
Cluster merge cascade at 8+ members
Constellation density for mass-death graves
Bond age pulse rate slows as bond ages
visual_verification: Dissolution phase water takes on warm amber tone. Night phases show dark indigo water matching the night sky. Lush biome water reflects the blue-cyan zenith strongly. Star columns create twinkling reflections in still night water.
quality_delta: mote brightness 0.50 -> 0.54 (floor 105->115). Water reflection 0.28 shallow -> 0.58*reflPhase; peak at golden hour 0.51.

title: ancient bond mourning
reflection: Bonds alive 70s+ now get a distinct farewell: slow amber expanding ring visually, deep sustained chord aurally. Each biome has its own sound for the loss.
looking_ahead: Cluster merge cascade at 8+ members
Constellation density for mass-death graves
Bond age pulse rate slows as bond ages
Mote curiosity — facing toward nearest unbonded neighbor
visual_verification: Tundra biome screenshots show no regressions. The amber ring is a transient 1.4s effect visible during dissolution when long bonds finally break — confirmed in code: 24-point ring expands r=3→14 at 200→0 alpha.
quality_delta: No regressions. Avg brightness 0.55 before, 0.53 after (different biome/seed). No clumping. No quality issues.

title: biome ambient life
reflection: Each biome gets organic atmosphere during organization/complexity: lush=fireflies, volcanic=embers, tundra=ice sparkles, temperate=pollen, desert=heat dust. Absent outside phases 1–3.
looking_ahead: Synchronized cluster heartbeat — bonded motes pulse in unison
Firefly audio — soft clicking chirps in lush biome
Cluster merge cascade at 8+ members
Bond age pulse slows as bond ages
visual_verification: Tundra biome complexity screenshot shows scattered ice-blue glints in the air above terrain. Organization phase shows fewer, exploration even fewer. Dissolution and silence have no ambient particles as designed.
quality_delta: Avg visible count 7.4→18.7 (analyzer detects particles as motes). Clumping 0.80→0.00. Brightness 0.51→0.50 (particles dilute mote avg, benign).

title: cluster heartbeat
reflection: Bonded motes now pulse in unison — larger clusters beat slower. At each peak, a brief corona crowns every cluster member. Brightness floor raised to fix dim-mote quality warning.
looking_ahead: Firefly audio — soft clicking chirps in lush biome
Cluster merge cascade audio at 8+ members
Bond age pulse rate slows as bond ages
Mote curiosity — facing toward nearest unbonded neighbor
visual_verification: Dissolution-mid shows motes clearly lit along terrain with good contrast against dark sky. Organization-mid shows the cluster identity ring active. Brightness 0.49→0.51 avg, silence phase 0.60. No quality warnings.
quality_delta: avgMoteBrightness 0.49→0.51. Brightness floor 115→125. MOTE_TOO_DIM warning resolved.

title: social proximity arcs
reflection: Approaching motes now show faint animated dotted arcs — social gravity made visible before bonds form. Old bonds breathe slowly; new bonds pulse fast. Lush gets firefly chirps, tundra gets crystal pings.
looking_ahead: Cluster merge cascade at 8+ members
Mote curiosity — orient toward nearest unbonded neighbor
Constellation density for mass-death graves
Bond age pulse further differentiation — ancient bonds almost still
visual_verification: Organization/complexity screenshots show faint dotted gray wisps between nearby motes that haven't bonded yet. No regressions on brightness or clumping. Bond lines visibly animate at different rates by age.
quality_delta: No regressions. Avg visible motes 8.6→16.6 (different seed/timing). Clumping 0.00 (worst 0.17 in silence, benign).

title: curiosity, birth, cascade
reflection: Motes now face their bond targets. Birth fires a 22px light lance from terrain. Clusters of 8+ trigger a triple expanding ring burst.
looking_ahead: Bond age pulse further differentiation — ancient bonds almost still
Constellation density for mass-death graves
Cascade audio — rising arpeggio reaching full chord at 8+
Mote jump/hop when blocked by cliff — traversal feels intentional
visual_verification: Organization-mid and complexity screenshots show dramatic multicolored expanding rings from large cluster centroids. Volcanic biome motes clearly visible as bright colored sparks.
quality_delta: avgMoteBrightness 0.56→0.52 (different biome seed, not a regression). Clumping 0.04→0.00. No quality warnings.

title: cluster beacons, graveyard rings
reflection: Large clusters (6+) now emit soft colored light pillars rising into the sky. Silence phase shows memorial rings at sites where communities died together.
looking_ahead: Mote curiosity — orient toward nearest unbonded neighbor
Cluster merge cascade at 8+ members
Bond age pulse further differentiation — ancient bonds almost still
Beacon color shifts with cluster energy — dying clusters flicker red
visual_verification: Organization/complexity screenshots show soft jellyfish-like glow columns rising from mote clusters. Beacon visible above terrain in phases 2-4. Silence graveyard rings appear for death clusters of 4+. Avg brightness 0.50→0.56.
quality_delta: avgMoteBrightness 0.50→0.56. Clumping 0.04→0.00. No regressions.

title: soul wisps
reflection: Each death now leaves a soft glowing spirit that drifts 22px above the terrain and persists for 50 seconds. By dissolution the sky fills with the souls of everyone who walked this world.
looking_ahead: Cascade audio — rising arpeggio reaching full chord at 8+
Mote jump/hop when blocked by cliff
Soul wisps cluster together near graveyard sites
Wisp color deepens with time — freshest souls brightest
visual_verification: 10x-speed complexity/dissolution screenshots show soft colored orbs floating above the mote terrain line. Sky visibly populated with lingering spirits. Empty sky in before comparison confirms the change.
quality_delta: Avg brightness 0.55 (no regression). No clumping. New system adds sky depth during complexity and dissolution phases.

title: flood storm, brighter wisps
reflection: Flood events now bring dark storm clouds, heavy diagonal rain, and a thunder flash. Soul wisp max-alpha doubled (28→60) so the dead are unmistakably present.
looking_ahead: Cascade audio — rising arpeggio reaching full chord at 8+
Drought visual — bleached sky, heat shimmer amplified, cracked earth
Soul wisps cluster near graveyard sites
Mote hop/jump when blocked by cliff face
visual_verification: After screenshots show dissolution sky populated with visible glowing soul orbs above terrain. Flood storm confirmed in code: sky darkening gradient, 70 diagonal rain streaks, thunder flash at event start. No regressions in quality analysis.
quality_delta: avgMoteBrightness 0.50→0.52. Clumping 0.00. Water coverage 10.8%→20.8% (different biome, not from changes).

title: cascade arpeggio, visible clouds
reflection: Cluster cascade now triggers a rising 6-note arpeggio that resolves to a sustained chord. Cloud density tripled — skies have visible drifting puffs in all weather.
looking_ahead: Drought visual — bleached sky, heat shimmer amplified
Soul wisps cluster near graveyard sites
God rays strengthened — currently too faint to see
Phase-reactive clouds — build through complexity, clear in silence
visual_verification: After screenshots show visible grey cloud shapes in the daytime sky above terrain — sky is no longer a flat empty gradient. Organization/complexity phases have clear cloud texture.
quality_delta: avgMoteBrightness 0.52→0.52. Brightness floor 125→138. Cloud density 0.10-0.30→0.28-0.52. Cloud count (clear) 0-2→3-6. No regressions.

title: god rays and bird calls
reflection: God rays boosted 4x (alpha 26→110, length 42→68, 8→12 shafts). Birds now chirp every 8-20s with biome-specific calls — songbird, tropical trill, tundra whistle, hawk cry.
looking_ahead: God rays fade through cloud density — shaft gaps when overcast
Birds react to storms — scatter visually when weather turns
Drought visual — bleached sky, heat shimmer amplified
Sunrise/sunset rays warm from white to gold as sun nears horizon
visual_verification: Volcanic biome in captures absorbs amber rays (intentional — ash filters). In temperate/desert biomes the shafts are clearly visible streaks spanning ~68px from sun. Before max alpha 26, after max alpha ~110.
quality_delta: No regressions. avgMoteBrightness 0.53 stable. God ray strength 4x. Bird chirps audible every 8-20s.

title: bird flocks in sky
reflection: Added pixel bird flocks that cross the sky during exploration through dissolution. Phase-reactive: absent at genesis/silence, peak at complexity. Biome silhouette colors differ per world.
looking_ahead: Birds react to storms — scatter or shelter when weather turns
Seasonal migration: flocks grow larger at complexity, dwindle at dissolution
Birds perch briefly on high terrain features before departing
Sound: faint wingbeat or distant call during active flock phases
visual_verification: Frame 04 (complexity) shows many dark V-shaped specks scattered across sky. Frame 11 (organization) has a distinct flock cluster visible upper-right. Genesis frame correctly empty of birds.
quality_delta: No metric regressions. avgMoteBrightness 0.54 stable. Birds are additive pixels only.

title: sunset sky and milky way
reflection: Dissolution now paints the sky blazing orange at the horizon transitioning to deep purple at zenith. Water reflections warm to amber. Night phases get a faint Milky Way band.
looking_ahead: Birds scatter/shelter during storms
Drought visual — bleached sky, heat shimmer amplified
Soul wisps cluster near graveyard sites
Sunrise more dramatic — wider orange sweep at exploration start
visual_verification: Dissolution screenshots show deep burgundy-to-purple gradient sky above volcanic lava. Silence phase blazes orange-crimson near horizon. Before: flat teal/blue sky. After: unmistakable sunset arc.
quality_delta: avgMoteBrightness 0.52 stable. No regressions. Clumping 0.00.

title: dissolution wind storm
reflection: Dissolution now brings 55 horizontal wind streaks blowing across the sky plus progressive sky darkening from above. The phase arc is unmistakable: calm golden complexity → turbulent stormy dissolution.
looking_ahead: Birds scatter during dissolution storm — flock turbulence visually
Drought visual — bleached sky, heat shimmer amplified, cracked ground
Soul wisps cluster near graveyard sites
Sunrise more dramatic — wider orange sweep at exploration start
visual_verification: Dissolution-phase screenshot shows horizontal pale wind streaks in the purple-orange sky, sky darkening at zenith. Before: flat warm golden sky. After: dark stormy atmosphere with visible directional wind motion.
quality_delta: avgMoteBrightness stable 0.51→0.50. No regressions. Clumping 0.02 worst 0.11 dissolution (benign).

title: dissolution rain, silence frost
reflection: Dissolution brings light diagonal rain (temperate/lush/tundra) — the world cries as it ends. Silence terrain desaturates to cold blue-silver moonlight.
looking_ahead: Birds scatter during dissolution storm — flock turbulence
Drought visual — bleached sky, heat shimmer amplified
Soul wisps cluster near graveyard sites
Rain intensifies near flood events — layered storm
visual_verification: Silence-mid screenshot shows SILENCE label confirmed with visibly cooler, more blue terrain tiles. Dissolution frames show the existing wind streaks gaining the new rain layer in eligible biomes.
quality_delta: avgMoteBrightness stable 0.51. No regressions. Clumping 0.01 (max 0.06, benign). silence moonlight depth 3px→8px, lightStr 0.38→0.42-0.62.

title: flock scatter, lone bird
reflection: Dissolution flocks panic and scatter as wind tears through — formations break, birds flee in seeded random directions. Silence gets one lone survivor gliding slowly across the empty sky.
looking_ahead: Drought visual — bleached sky, heat shimmer amplified, cracked ground
Soul wisps cluster near graveyard sites
Rain intensifies near flood events — layered storm
Scatter audio — brief panicked calls as dissolution flock breaks
visual_verification: Silence-mid shows a distinct 7-pixel-wide bird shape drifting alone in mid-sky above the empty landscape. Dissolution frames show birds mid-scatter with faster panic flap speed. No regressions.
quality_delta: avgMoteBrightness 0.52→0.53. Clumping 0.00. No regressions.
